Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to go through each case one by one compare their activity sequences to the normative rules then list the violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: all cases start with Receive order then must do Check inventory next the prompt says after an order is received the next logical step is to check inventory so Check inventory has to come after Receive and before any payment authorization or fulfillment steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: two main paths sufficient inventory path Receive Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order and insufficient inventory path Receive Check inventory Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: general rules Authorize payment only after Check inventory before packaging Package only after authorize before ship Ship only after package never after cancel Close is always last Any deviation is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close exactly the sufficient inventory path all steps in order no extra steps Close is last Case 1 is compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 sequence is Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close The rule says Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Here Package comes before Authorize payment That is a violation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Also wait is that the only violation Lets see Ship is after Package which is okay Close is last Check is after Receive So the only violation in Case 2 is the order of Authorize and Package

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close After Cancel order they did Ship order The prompt says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Oh another thing there is no Package step before Ship in Case 3 The rule says Ship order should only occur after packaging So Case 3 has Ship with no Package that is another violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: since a cancellation occurred indicating insufficient inventory the intended path after Check inventory is to proceed directly to Cancel order but the case includes an Authorize payment step between Check and Cancel which deviates from the specified insufficient inventory flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 sequence is Receive Check Cancel Close that is exactly the insufficient inventory path all steps in order no extra steps Close is last Case 4 is compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 sequence is Receive Check Authorize Ship Close no Package items between Authorize and Ship The rule says Package items should occur after authorization and before shipping so Case 5 is missing the Package step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets list all non compliant cases first Case 2 Case 3 and Case 5 Cases 1 and 4 are compliant
